business.headline: Why PM is asking Indians to cut spending: NITI Aayog explains austerity messageAs tensions in West Asia continue to rise, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged Indians to be careful with spending and avoid unnecessary expenses. But why now? Is this simply a precautionary step or a sign of deeper economic worries? NITI Aayog Vice Chairman Ashok Lahiri explains what the Prime Minister's austerity message really means.

Damage is now evident: Market strategist Akshay Chinchalkar on oil, gold, rupeeIn an interaction with IndiaToday.in, Akshay Chinchalkar, Managing Partner and Head of Markets Strategy at The Wealth Company, explains rising market stress, rupee weakness, soaring gold prices and investor fears.

If not physical gold, then what? Why experts are backing ETFs as an alternativeWith gold prices surging after the import duty hike, investors are increasingly turning towards ETFs as a cheaper, more flexible alternative to physical bullion amid rising uncertainty and rupee weakness.

Why Trump took Musk, Tim Cook, Jensen Huang, other top CEOs to ChinaTravelling with US President Trump are some of the world's biggest corporate names, including Elon Musk, Jensen Huang, Tim Cook and Larry Fink, along with executives from companies such as Meta, Visa, Goldman Sachs, Boeing and Mastercard.

Why did India ban sugar exports? Here's what's happeningThe ban applies to exports of raw, white and refined sugar, although shipments already in the export pipeline under specific conditions will still be allowed. Certain quota-based exports to the US and European Union remain exempt.
Sensex jumps over 300 points, Nifty above 23,500; Airtel up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 391.36 points to 75,000.34, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 148.65 points to 23,561.25 as of 9:36 am.

Sensex, Nifty today: Will stock market crash or open high today?Dalal Street is set for a higher opening after a mild rebound, with GIFT Nifty pointing above Wednesday's close. The recovery remains tentative as a record-low rupee, high crude prices, persistent foreign selling and elevated volatility keep sentiment fragile.

Gold gets costlier after PM Modi's appeal. Is petrol, diesel next?After the government raised gold import duty, fresh attention has turned to petrol and diesel prices as crude stays high. Remarks by PM Narendra Modi, the RBI and the IMF have sharpened concerns that consumers may not stay protected indefinitely.

Govt bans sugar exports till September to cool domestic prices, risks global crisisThe decision comes as authorities try to prevent a rise in domestic sugar prices at a time when food inflation remains politically and economically sensitive for Indian households.

Rupee depreciation matter of concern, but no need to get alarmed about it: Ex-RBI chiefIn an exclusive interview with India Today, former Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Governor Duvvuri Subbarao said that the rupee's depreciation is a matter of concern, but there is no need to get alarmed about it.

From 4-day work week to higher PF: 11 big changes under the new labour codeIndia's new labour code could change how millions work and get paid, with possible changes to office hours, overtime rules, salary structure and gratuity benefits. Here are 11 important changes employees should know.

Amul, Mother Dairy hike milk prices by Rs 2 per litre nationwide from May 14Your morning tea, coffee or glass of milk may soon feel a little heavier on the pocket. Amul and Mother Dairy have announced a fresh increase in milk prices across India, with the revised rates coming into effect from May 14.

Insurance claim rejected? Reddit user shares how this family fought back and wonMost people stop fighting after an insurance company rejects a claim. One Reddit user, however, shared how a family challenged a rejection linked to a pre-existing condition, spotted an error, escalated the matter, and finally won.

Sensex, Nifty recover after 4-day slide even as rupee hits record lowThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 49.74 points, or 0.07%, to close at 74,608.98, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 33.05 points, or 0.14%, to settle at 23,412.60.

RBI Governor says petrol, diesel price hike likely if West Asia crisis drags onRBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra said India may have to raise petrol and diesel prices if the West Asia crisis persists. The remarks highlight growing pressure on inflation, fuel imports and state-run oil companies absorbing higher crude costs.

Gold duty hike explained: What happens to your purchases now?Gold prices were already high, and now a fresh duty hike has made them even steeper. With jewellery stores expected to revise prices in the coming days, one big question remains, i.e., how will this impact your next purchase?

Should you buy jewellery stocks now or wait after the customs duty hike?The customs duty hike on gold and silver has rattled jewellery stocks, with concerns rising over weaker demand and higher costs. With shares of major players turning volatile, investors are now asking a key question: is this a buying opportunity or a signal to wait?

Tata Power shares crashed over 6% today. Should you buy?Tata Power shares fell more than 6% after weaker-than-expected March quarter earnings and mixed brokerage calls. The reaction spotlighted valuation concerns even as the company's renewable and solar businesses continued to grow.

Customs duty rises on gold, silver: Will buying slow or stay strong?Gold and silver have turned costlier after the government raised customs duty. The move has immediately sparked questions in bullion markets. Will higher prices cool demand, or will buying continue to stay strong despite the hike?
Sensex opens 100 points higher, Nifty above 23,400; Asian Paints up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex was down 216.67 points to 74,342.57, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 68.50 points to 23,311.05 as of 9:47 am.

entertainment.headline: Suriya's Karuppu releases in Pune amid delay in South states, videos go viral Suriya's Karuppu released in Pune after its morning shows were cancelled across several southern states over unpaid dues. The patchy rollout has put focus on the film's pending payments.

Bridgerton Season 5 production begins; Francesca-Michaela's love story set for 2027 Bridgerton Season 5 will return in 2027 with Season 5, ending its two-year release pattern. The new chapter centres on Francesca Bridgerton and Michaela Stirling as production begins in the UK.

Krishnavatharam director Hardik Gajjar says he was wary of portraying Raas Leela Krishnavatharam director Hardik Gajjar said portraying Radha-Krishna's Raas Leela was his toughest task. The sequence is now drawing strong audience response for balancing romance with dignity.

KPop Demon Hunters brings Huntr/x to life with new world concert tour Netflix and AEG Presents have announced a global concert tour for KPop Demon Hunters. Here's what is known so far about the live shows, the film's success and why fans are already waiting for tickets.

BTS, Madonna, Shakira set for FIFA World Cup final halftime show The 2026 FIFA World Cup final is set to make history with its first-ever halftime show featuring global superstars BTS, Madonna, and Shakira. The event is to take place on July 19.

King set leak shows Abhishek Bachchan with shotgun in villain avatar Actor Abhishek Bachchan's look from King has leaked online as filming continues in Mumbai after the South Africa schedule. The image has surfaced despite director Siddharth Anand's appeal against sharing visuals from the set.

CM Vijay to work on Jana Nayagan release, calls producer back from Cannes After taking office as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister, Joseph Vijay has reopened efforts to secure clearance for Jana Nayagan and asked producer Venkat to return to Chennai. The move puts his stalled farewell film back in focus.

Belly and Conrad are back: First photo from The Summer I Turned Pretty film out The first official picture of Belly and Conrad from The Summer I Turned Pretty film is finally here, confirming that the movie is officially in production. The update comes after the team issued a message asking fans to respect the cast and crew's privacy during filming.

Explained: Why Suriya-Trisha's Karuppu shows were cancelled at the last minute Suriya and Trisha Krishnan's Karuppu missed its planned 9 am release after last-minute financial and distribution issues. The delay left theatres shut, fans frustrated and the film's same-day release dependent on dues being cleared.

I get lot of work because I'm Aamir Khan's son, says Junaid after two flops Junaid Khan admitted that being Aamir Khan's son helped him in getting film offers despite the failure of Loveyapa and Ek Din. The actor also shared his views on nepotism, saying producers prefer actors they can market easily.

Absolutely iconic: Alia Bhatt gushes over Aishwarya Rai's Devdas Cannes look Actor Alia Bhatt said Aishwarya Rai Bachchan's look at the premiere of Devdas at the 2002 Cannes Film Festival remains her most memorable appearance. Her remark has renewed attention on Aishwarya's long legacy at the film festival.

Punjab's global star, not Indian? Diljit Dosanjh's US citizenship report sparks buzz A report says Diljit Dosanjh acquired US citizenship in 2022 and has travelled on a US passport since September that year. The disclosure has drawn attention because he recently declined calls to enter Punjab politics and is closely identified with Punjab.

Perfection: Neetu Kapoor reacts to Alia Bhatt's Cannes 2026 red carpet look Alia Bhatt continues to grab attention with her stunning Cannes appearance, which has now been praised by her mother-in-law Neetu Kapoor. She called the actor's look "perfection."

Vin Diesel tears up remembering Paul Walker, hugs his daughter Meadow at Cannes Actor Vin Diesel became emotional while remembering Paul Walker during a Cannes screening of The Fast and the Furious. The tribute, joined by Meadow Walker and the cast, also framed the franchise's road to its 2028 finale.

Suriya-Trisha's Karuppu 9 am shows cancelled as financial woes continue Suriya and Trisha's Karuppu missed its scheduled 9 am shows hours before release after financial issues persisted. The last-minute disruption disappointed fans and clouded the film's opening plans.

The Last Tenant: Sarthak recalls directing Irrfan in a film lost 25 years ago Sarthak Dasgupta has released his long-lost short film The Last Tenant, starring Irrfan and Vidya Balan, on YouTube. The rediscovered film turned the release into a cathartic tribute that drew an overwhelming response.

Watch: Alia Bhatt channels modern maharani in a regal gown at Cannes 2026 Alia Bhatt stepped out at a Cannes evening event in an off-shoulder gown with heritage-inspired detailing. The appearance added another distinct fashion moment to her varied run at the festival.

Alia Bhatt meets F1 racer Charles Leclerc at Cannes. Video and fan reactions Actor Alia Bhatt was seen greeting Formula 1 driver Charles Leclerc at a L'Oreal event at the Cannes Film Festival. The clip spread quickly online, with fans also linking it to her earlier meeting with Carlos Sainz.

Mouni Roy steps out in Mumbai amid separation rumours with Suraj Nambiar Actor Mouni Roy was seen leaving a restaurant in Mumbai as speculation around her marriage to entrepreneur Suraj Nambiar intensified. The outing came hours after she asked media outlets and social media users to stop spreading false narratives and respect their privacy.

general news.headline: Congress names VD Satheesan as Kerala Chief Minister after 10 days of suspenseThe Congress has named VD Satheesan as Kerala's next Chief Minister after 10 days of deliberations, choosing the popular six-time MLA over KC Venugopal and Ramesh Chennithala.

Mamata Banerjee, in lawyer's gown, at court to argue post-poll violence caseThe case pertains to allegations of attacks on political workers and party offices following the high-stakes elections that ended the Trinamool Congress's 15-year rule and brought the BJP to power in the state.

Party is supreme, says Congress's KC Venugopal after being passed over for Kerala CMKC Venugopal welcomed the Congress high command's decision to appoint VD Satheesan as Kerala Chief Minister, reaffirming that the party and its workers are supreme to him.

Must address unilateral coercive steps, sanctions inconsistent with law: JaishankarHe said these developments are not isolated events but interconnected challenges that are straining multilateral institutions and worsening vulnerabilities for developing nations.

SIR gets kicking: Bengal, Bihar to bar deleted names from social welfare schemesThe Election Commission-led SIR exercise has now expanded beyond polls to plugging holes in direct benefit transfers. West Bengal and Bihar, two BJP-led states, will be using SIR data to identify duplicate, deceased and ineligible beneficiaries to plug leakages in welfare delivery systems.

VD Satheesan: 6-time MLA and grassroots worker who predicted 100+ seats for UDFVD Satheesan has been elected Kerala's chief minister, putting him at the centre of the state's new government. The result turns the spotlight on a six-time MLA and grassroots worker who had predicted 100-plus seats for the UDF.

Woman gang-raped inside bus in Delhi; driver, conductor arrestedA woman has alleged that a sleeper bus driver and conductor raped her inside the vehicle in Delhi. Police sources said the incident was reported to have taken place on May 12 and both men were arrested.

CM Vijay to work on Jana Nayagan release, calls producer back from CannesAfter taking office as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister, Joseph Vijay has reopened efforts to secure clearance for Jana Nayagan and asked producer Venkat to return to Chennai. The move puts his stalled farewell film back in focus.

The internet is finally waking up to the beauty of Odia cuisineOdia cuisine is one of the most underrated, but with time, it is slowly getting its due. The most recent acknowledgement came from industrialist Harsh Goenka.

Suriya-Trisha's Karuppu 9 am shows cancelled as financial woes continueSuriya and Trisha's Karuppu missed its scheduled 9 am shows hours before release after financial issues persisted. The last-minute disruption disappointed fans and clouded the film's opening plans.

How Maharashtra plans to protect a dam, local fishermen from alien fish speciesA multi-stage intervention will reintroduce native freshwater species like the Deccan Mahseer into the Ujani dam reservoir, boosting fishing incomes
The Man Who Will Not Be a Rubber StampIn Delhi, Rahul Gandhi refuses to be a rubber stamp. In Kerala, he is making a rubber stamp out of MLAs elected by the people.

What was the thundersquall that killed over 89 in UP? Science explainedA violent thundersquall killed at least 89 people across five districts of Uttar Pradesh on May 13, collapsing homes and uprooting trees in Bhadohi, Fatehpur, and Budaun.

What happened to Prateek Yadav? Doctor, autopsy reveal hidden health battlePrateek Yadav's doctor said he left the ICU against medical advice despite repeated warnings. His postmortem later attributed his death to massive pulmonary thromboembolism, ruling out steroid speculation.

Mishandling Taiwan could push US-China ties into dangerous point, Xi warns Trump Live UpdatesXi Jinping described Taiwan as the most critical issue in relations between Beijing and Washington and warned Donald Trump that mishandling the matter could damage bilateral ties.

Influencer 'The Skin Doctor' arrested for posts targeting Sunjay Kapur's familyDelhi Police arrested 'The Skin Doctor' for allegedly posting objectionable content targeting late industrialist Sunjay Kapur's family.
A day in the life of health nerdsHandcrafted ByOur Editors They optimise everything: Sleep, food, workouts and recovery. Meet the health nerds.

Emmanuel Macron's wife slapped him over messages with Iranian actor, book claimsA new book claimed that Brigitte Macron slapped French President Emmanuel Macron after allegedly discovering messages with actress Golshifteh Farahani on his phone.

US, China should be partners, not rivals: Xi's measured message for TrumpDonald Trump opened talks with Xi Jinping in Beijing as both sides took up trade and regional flashpoints. The summit comes at a delicate moment, with Iran, Taiwan and technology tensions shaping the agenda.

Punjab's global star, not Indian? Diljit Dosanjh's US citizenship report sparks buzzA report says Diljit Dosanjh acquired US citizenship in 2022 and has travelled on a US passport since September that year. The disclosure has drawn attention because he recently declined calls to enter Punjab politics and is closely identified with Punjab.
Alia Bhatt wears a corset gown by Tarun Tahiliani at Cannes dinnerAlia Bhatt stepped out for L'Oréal Paris dinner in a custom Tarun Tahiliani gown inspired by archival chintz florals.

Live: Om Birla, Bihar, Andhra, Haryana CMs cut convoys amid fuel savings pushPM's forex-saving push Live Updates: Amid a global oil supply crunch and rising fuel prices, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged citizens and government departments to cut unnecessary expenditure and focus on savings. Stressing the need for economic resilience during a period of energy uncertainty, the Prime Minister called for prudent fuel consumption and the efficient use of resources to reduce the impact of the ongoing oil crisis on households and the broader economy. Following this, state governments and multiple departments have begun chalking out directives in this direction. Stay tuned to India Today as we bring you the latest developments.

Cisco lays off 4,000 employees, CEO says must take hard decisions in AI eraAmerican tech firm Cisco has announced that it plans to lay off just under 4,000 workers. Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins wrote in an internal memo that the company needed to make hard decisions to win in the "AI era."

Top virologist flags preparedness gaps in MV Hondius hantavirus outbreakDr Shahid Jameel said the hantavirus response linked to the MV Hondius outbreak appears appropriate. He said the episode highlights global gaps in surveillance and the need for sustained preparedness.

sports.headline: Axar Patel, Rahane, Pant face IPL captaincy axe after disastrous campaigns Axar Patel, Rishabh Pant and Ajinkya Rahane could lose their captaincy after poor IPL campaigns, sources tracking franchise developments told PTI. The scrutiny has sharpened as playoff hopes fade and teams assess leadership, selections and coaching support.

Virat Kohli's century proves old guard still runs the show, says Sunil Gavaskar Sunil Gavaskar hailed Virat Kohli's unbeaten century against KKR as proof that the old guard still has plenty to offer, with the RCB star silencing doubters, smashing records and punishing KKR for an early dropped chance.

Pat Cummins slams rumours of leaving BBL for SA20 over payment disparity Pat Cummins has rubbished reports suggesting Australian players may prioritise SA20 over the BBL from 2028, as the debate over franchise pay gaps and Cricket Australia's contract structure intensifies.

Hardik will leave MI after IPL 2026: Ex-India captain makes bold prediction Kris Srikkanth has claimed Hardik Pandya will leave Mumbai Indians after IPL 2026. MI have struggled under Hardik's captaincy and suffered an early exit from IPL 2026.

After Sreejesh's fiery exit, Hockey India appoint Soyez as Jr.team coach Hockey India appointed Frederic Soyez as coach of the junior men's team a day after PR Sreejesh's exit from the role. Soyez comes in with a big reputation, having worked as the head coach of Spain and France before.

If you drop Virat Kohli in a chase, bound to get difficult: Abhishek Nayar RCB vs KKR: Abhishek Nayar admitted KKR paid the price for dropping Virat Kohli, while also pointing to Varun Chakravarthy's absence as a crucial factor in the six-wicket defeat to RCB.

Told you so: Krunal Pandya's prediction for Virat Kohli comes true in Raipur RCB vs KKR: Krunal Pandya's faith in Virat Kohli paid off spectacularly as the RCB star bounced back from back-to-back ducks with a match-winning century against KKR in Raipur. Kohli's stunning knock not only powered RCB to the top of the table but also shattered multiple T20 records.

PBKS vs MI, Dharamshala weather: Will rain spoil Punjab's playoff chances? Punjab Kings and Mumbai Indians head to Dharamsala with both sides short on momentum. For Punjab, the bigger concern is not just rain but a campaign wobbling at the wrong time.

PBKS vs MI: Punjab Kings can't stop the rumours. They seek to stop the rot PBKS vs MI, IPL 2026 preview: Punjab Kings have answered the noise on social media, backed their players and drawn a line. Now, at a rain-soaked Dharamsala, the only statement that actually matters is the one they make on the field against Mumbai Indians on Thursday.

City smash Palace to keep pressure on Arsenal in Premier League title race Manchester City breezed past Crystal Palace at home to keep pressure on Arsenal in the Premier League title race on Wednesday, May 13. City cut the gap to Arsenal to two points.

Virat Kohli's double act: When the human and the superhuman showed up in Raipur IPL 2026, RCB vs KKR: After back-to-back ducks, Virat Kohli walked out in Raipur nervous, celebrated a single like it was a six, and then scored a match-winning hundred. One night, two sides of the most compelling cricketer of his generation were on full display.

IPL Play of the Day: Kohli, Padikkal return as RCB's perfect chase comboRCB vs KKR: Whenever Virat Kohli and Devdutt Padikkal settle into an IPL 2026 chase together, panic slowly disappears from the RCB dugout. One controls the tempo, the other destroys the pressure, and Bengaluru suddenly make big run chases look far easier than they should.

Virat Kohli's special night lights up Raipur with plethora of records: In StatsIPL 2026, RCB vs KKR: Virat Kohli smashed a sensational century and shattered multiple records as Royal Challengers Bengaluru climbed to the top of the table with a six-wicket win over Kolkata Knight Riders on Wednesday in Raipur.

Virat Kohli no longer chasing records, just soaking in cricket before it endsIPL 2026: Virat Kohli struck an unbeaten 105 as RCB beat KKR by six wickets in Raipur. After reaching multiple milestones, he said he now wants to savour every day left in cricket.

Catch of the season? Manish Pandey leaves Virat Kohli, KKR teammates stunnedIPL 2026: Manish Pandey pulled off a one-handed diving catch to dismiss Tim David during RCB's chase in Raipur. Virat Kohli's century and RCB's six-wicket win still left KKR facing a tougher qualification path.

Virat Kohli roars back in landmark clash to dent Kolkata's playoffs hopes badlyIPL 2026, RCB vs KKR: Virat Kohli had a dream day as he scored a hundred and completed 14000 T20 runs as Royal Challengers Bengaluru beat the Kolkata Knight Riders by six wickets on Wednesday in Raipur.

CSK's playoff-push at risk after Jamie Overton flies back to UK due to injuryIPL 2026: Jamie Overton will return to the UK after suffering a right thigh injury. His exit leaves Chennai Super Kings without a key all-round option in a crucial playoff phase.

Virat Kohli ends duck streak with animated celebration in RCB vs KKRIPL 2026: Virat Kohli ended his run of ducks with an animated first-run celebration against Kolkata Knight Riders in Raipur. The moment capped his record 279th IPL appearance and eased the focus on his brief slump.

Why did Varun Chakravarthy miss the game vs RCB? Head coach Abhishek Nayyar revealsIPL 2026: Abhishek Nayyar said Varun Chakravarthy missed KKR's game against RCB Iin Raipur after suffering a hairline inury. The injury is on his landing foot, leaving the franchise hopeful rather than certain about his return next match.

Sarah Taylor breaks new ground as England men's first-ever female coachSarah Taylor has become the first woman to join the England men's coaching setup, adding another historic milestone to her trailblazing cricket journey after successful stints with Sussex, Team Abu Dhabi, England Lions and Gujarat Giants.

Real Madrid lose again! This time in court over homophobic chants against GuardiolaCAS upheld UEFA's sanctions after Real Madrid challenged punishment over homophobic chants aimed at Pep Guardiola. The ruling strengthens football's harder line on discrimination and leaves Madrid with another off-field defeat.

Venus Williams, 45, returns to French Open after 5 years, set to play doublesFrench Open 2026: At 45, Venus Williams is set to return to Roland Garros after five years, partnering rising American star Hailey Baptiste in the women's doubles event.

IPL 2026: Varun Chakravarthy injury scare grows as KKR spinner misses RCB clashIPL 2026: Varun Chakravarthy missed KKR's must-win IPL 2026 match against RCB in Raipur, with Saurabh Dubey replacing him. The late change raised fresh doubts over the spinner's fitness and sharpened the stakes for both teams.

Who is Saurabh Dubey? KKR hand IPL debut to Vidarbha left-arm pacer vs RCBKolkata Knight Riders handed Vidarbha left-arm pacer Saurabh Dubey his IPL debut against Royal Challengers Bengaluru after Varun Chakaravarthy was ruled out with an injury.

Italian Open: Sublime Iga Swiatek steamrolls Pegula to reach semifinalsThree-time Italian Open champion Iga Swiatek dismantled American fifth seed Jessica Pegula 6-1, 6-2 to storm into the semi-finals of the WTA 1000 clay-court tournament in Rome on Wednesday.

Punjab Kings pushback before reset: Bloggers can say anything, we are not concernedIPL 2026, PBKS vs MI: After defending their dressing room against a digital riot of rumours, Punjab Kings look to tap into the calm in Dharamsala to shift focus on their push to the playoffs. Having lost four on the bounce, the home team will take on Mumbai Indians on Thursday.

Hockey India denies PR Sreejesh firing claims, says Olympic hero rejected new roleHockey India has denied PR Sreejesh's claim that he was removed as junior men's coach for a foreign replacement. The exchange has widened a debate over trust in Indian coaches despite his strong results with the side.

Neeraj Chopra gets Switzerland camp approval before CWG and Asian GamesThe Mission Olympic Cell has approved Neeraj Chopra's 47-day training camp in Switzerland and cleared foreign exposure trips for Manu Bhaker and Gulveer Singh. The plans come as India readies key medal contenders for the Commonwealth Games and Asian Games after a demanding season.

Women's T20 World Cup: India to face England, West Indies in warm-up matchesThe ICC has confirmed India's warm-up schedule ahead of the ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026, with Harmanpreet Kaur's side set to face West Indies and hosts England before the main tournament begins.

Ahmedabad set to host FIBA U18 Asia Cup as India's basketball push grows biggerAhmedabad will host the FIBA U18 Asia Cup 2026 from August 13 to 23, with 16 teams competing for the continental title and U19 World Cup places. The event brings a World Cup qualification tournament to India for the first time in 22 years and adds basketball to the city's expanding multi-sport portfolio.

Kagiso Rabada left fascinated by 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's batting approachIPL 2026: Kagiso Rabada has praised 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after the Rajasthan Royals batter attacked Gujarat Titans without hesitation in the IPL. The South African said the teenager's fast hands and fearlessness make him an X-factor while reinforcing the value of orthodox fast bowling in T20 cricket.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
